channel.title: Pratik Dhanave — Blog
channel.description: Long-form writing on multi-agent AI, medical AI governance, HIPAA-aware architecture, and cloud-native systems.

item.title: Lessons from Converting 18 Agents in 90 Days
item.description: What worked, what was hard, and what we'd do differently. Real numbers: 18 agents, 90 days, 5 governance policies, 4 provider swaps.

item.title: Deployment & A2A: From ADK's Web Deploy to MAF's Scalable Architecture
item.description: Cloud Run deployments, agent-to-agent communication, load balancing, and production observability.

item.title: Callbacks and Middleware: Porting ADK Hooks to MAF's Decorator Pattern
item.description: Callbacks to middleware: composable decorators for audit, retry, token enforcement, and OpenTelemetry integration.

item.title: Provider Abstraction: From Gemini-Only to Swappable LLMs
item.description: Zero-code provider swaps: Ollama (dev), OpenAI (staging), Azure Foundry (prod). Same agents, different models.

item.title: Tool Wrapping: From ADK Functions to MAF Governed Tools
item.description: From ADK functions to MAF governed tools. Adding policy enforcement, DLP, approval gates, and OPA integration.

item.title: Token Exchange Patterns: Porting Multi-Turn State from ADK to MAF
item.description: Sessions to threads: porting multi-turn state from ADK to MAF. Token budgeting, long-term memory, and conversation audit trails.

item.title: The Executor Pattern: ADK→MAF Conversion for Agentic Control Flow
item.description: How to port ADK's orchestration callbacks to MAF builders without losing control. The executor pattern: you own the loop.

item.title: Why We Migrated from Google ADK to Microsoft MARA
item.description: The philosophy, trade-offs, and what we learned converting 18+ agents in 3 months. Provider abstraction as the foundation for portable agents.

item.title: OpenTelemetry and Evaluation in Multi-Agent Workflows — the full production stack
item.description: How to instrument multi-agent systems with OpenTelemetry, propagate trace context across an in-memory bus, and build a layered evaluation pipeline — from real-time policy gates to async LLM-as-judge to SLO-based trust scoring. Everything I learned building Genie.

item.title: Running a HIPAA-Aware Multi-Agent Medical AI on GKE: A Field Map
item.description: Google's GKE AI infrastructure docs list ~40 integrations. Here's a field map of which ones actually matter when the workload is a HIPAA-aware multi-agent medical AI, and where the gaps sit.

item.title: The Right-to-Explanation Handler: GDPR Article 22 as a Go HTTP Endpoint
item.description: How a 200-line Go handler turns an audit log and an eval store into a regulator-friendly answer to

item.title: Time-bound privileged access — building the PCSE §1.4 PAM analog in Go
item.description: Request → N-eyes approve → window-of-time → automatic expiry, with every transition written to a hash-chained audit log. The package that closes Gap #1 from the PCSE map.

item.title: HL7 v2 in 2026: Why This 50-Year-Old Protocol Still Drives Healthcare Integration
item.description: Why HL7 v2 — a 50-year-old pipe-delimited protocol — still drives most US hospital ADT integrations in 2026, and what a clean Go parser looks like in ~300 lines.

item.title: Studying for the AIGP? Here's a Reference Implementation in Go
item.description: Studying for the IAPP AI Governance Professional credential? Here's an open-source Go codebase that demonstrates ~70% of the body of knowledge in working code.

item.title: "Enough to Reconstruct, Never Enough to Leak": The HIPAA Audit Log Design Problem
item.description: Enough to reconstruct, never enough to leak. The audit event schema problem under §164.312(b), and how to solve it without conflating the audit sink with the PHI sink.

item.title: The Fallback Is the Contract: Reliability Patterns for Clinical AI
item.description: Every LLM-backed agent in this platform has a deterministic rule-based fallback. The case always finalises. The fallback isn't a workaround — it's the contract.

item.title: Multi-Agent Systems in 5 Interfaces: A MARA Implementation Field Report
item.description: Five interfaces hold the whole platform together. The 30-line orchestrator closure that makes the rest of the architecture testable, auditable, and safe to evolve.

item.title: PostgreSQL Row-Level Security Is HIPAA Defense in Depth
item.description: PostgreSQL row-level security as HIPAA defence in depth. Why fail-open application filtering isn't enough, and how 'append-only at DB GRANTs' carries more of the §164.312(b) burden than people realise.

item.title: The 21st Century Cures Act, Expressed in Go
item.description: The 21st Century Cures Act §3060 CDS carve-out criterion 4 expressed as a code-level queue, lossless on reject, with audit-recorded reviewer rationale. Build it once, satisfy GDPR Article 22 for free.

item.title: Moving Diagnostic Accuracy 42.9% → 85.7% by Changing Two Files
item.description: How a single sprint of specialty-rule work — guided by a benchmark that wasn't afraid to print embarrassing numbers — turned a 'demo respiratory differential' into a five-condition rule-based diagnostic engine.
